nodes.context: Italian-American Mafia crime family based in St. Louis, Missouri.
Sicilian gang formed in 1915 by Vito Giannola, John Giannola, and Alphonse Palizzola.
Early Italian gang in St. Louis, active since 1910, led by Pasquale Santino then Carmelo Fresina.
Splinter group of the Green Ones led by Tony Russo.
Ethnic gang in St. Louis involved in bootlegging conflicts.

Allied with St. Louis crime family per infobox; St. Louis family declared independence from it.

edges.evidence_quote: Giannola attempted to take control of the bootlegging rackets in the area and fought with members of the Egan's Rats gang and Cuckoos Gang.
edges.context: Green Ones fought with Egan's Rats over bootlegging rackets.

edges.evidence_quote: Giannola attempted to take control of the bootlegging rackets in the area and fought with members of the Egan's Rats gang and Cuckoos Gang.
edges.context: Green Ones fought with Cuckoos over bootlegging rackets.

edges.evidence_quote: Fresina formed an alliance with splinter group of the Green Ones called the Russo gang led by Tony Russo.
edges.context: Pillow Gang allied with Russo Gang to battle the Green Ones.

edges.evidence_quote: The two gangs battled with the Green Ones until 1928, when the Russo gang lost too many members and blamed the Pillow gang for betraying them.
edges.context: Pillow Gang and Russo Gang battled the Green Ones.

edges.evidence_quote: The two gangs battled with the Green Ones until 1928, when the Russo gang lost too many members and blamed the Pillow gang for betraying them.
edges.context: Russo Gang and Pillow Gang battled the Green Ones.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1931, Fresina was murdered and members of the Pillow gang continued fighting with the Russo gang and the Green Ones.
edges.context: Russo Gang fought with Pillow Gang after the alliance broke.

edges.evidence_quote: Anthony Giordano became boss and declared independence from the Kansas City crime family.
edges.context: St. Louis family declared independence from Kansas City family.

edges.evidence_quote: In the 1970s, Giordano, along with Detroit mobsters Anthony Joseph Zerilli and Michael Polizzi, attempted to gain control of the Frontier Hotel and Casino in Las Vegas.
edges.context: St. Louis family cooperated with Detroit mobsters in a Las Vegas casino scheme.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Chicago Outfit , Cleveland crime family , Colorado crime family , Detroit Partnership , Kansas City crime family
edges.context: St. Louis family listed Chicago Outfit as an ally in infobox.
